{"key_event":"Tobacco Protest mobilizes bazaar–ulama coalition","reasoning":"The nationwide tobacco boycott, clerical coordination, petitions, and expanding press/telegraph networks moved civil society from weak toward moderate capacity, able to veto a major royal concession. Qajar state capacity remained weak: fiscal dependence, foreign leverage, and a weaker shah offset the modest customs centralization under Belgian officials.","society_change":1.2,"society_power":5.2,"state_change":-0.3,"state_power":3.2}
